mainmatter.p: For any dcpo \(A\), the following is a co-comma square in the posetal 2-category of dcpos, where \(\mathsf{L}\) takes a dcpo to its lift or Scott-open partial map classifier.
mainmatter.block:
Proof.
We fix another lax square of the following form:
We must show that there exists a unique morphism \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d}D\) factoring the square above through \({\left\{d_\bot\sqsubseteq d_A\right\}}\) as follows:
We will define \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d}D\) to take \(a^{?}:\mathsf{L}{A}\) to the least upper bound of the directed subset \(U = {\left\{d_\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{d_A a\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\subseteq D\). As this assignment evidently has the correct factorization property, it remains just to check that it is continuous. Letting \(V\) be a directed subset of \(\mathsf{L}{A}\), we compute: \[\begin{aligned} d\bigsqcup V & = \bigsqcup\,{\left( {\left\{d_\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid \bigsqcup V = \eta_A a\right\}} \right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid \eta_Aa\in V\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup{\left\{\bigsqcup {\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \mid a^{?}\in V\right\}} \\ & = \bigsqcup{\left\{da^{?}\mid a^{?}\in V\right\}} \end{aligned} \]
For uniqueness, suppose that we have two morphisms \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d,d'}D\) factoring our square in the sense described above and fix \(a^{?}:\mathsf{L}{A}\): \[\begin{aligned} d a^{?} & = d\bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{\eta_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d\,{\left(\eta_Aa\right)}\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d'\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d'\,{\left(\eta_Aa\right)}\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = d'\bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{\eta_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = d'a^{?} \end{aligned} \]

mainmatter.p: We will deduce the closure of pointed dcpos under connected colimits using general results about monads. If we can show the closure of lifted dcpos under coproducts, we will have established the cocompleteness of the category of pointed dcpos and bottom-preserving maps.
For any dcpo \(A\), the following is a co-comma square in the posetal 2-category of dcpos, where \(\mathsf{L}\) takes a dcpo to its lift or Scott-open partial map classifier.
mainmatter.block:
Proof.
We fix another lax square of the following form:
We must show that there exists a unique morphism \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d}D\) factoring the square above through \({\left\{d_\bot\sqsubseteq d_A\right\}}\) as follows:
We will define \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d}D\) to take \(a^{?}:\mathsf{L}{A}\) to the least upper bound of the directed subset \(U = {\left\{d_\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{d_A a\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\subseteq D\). As this assignment evidently has the correct factorization property, it remains just to check that it is continuous. Letting \(V\) be a directed subset of \(\mathsf{L}{A}\), we compute: \[\begin{aligned} d\bigsqcup V & = \bigsqcup\,{\left( {\left\{d_\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid \bigsqcup V = \eta_A a\right\}} \right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid \eta_Aa\in V\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup{\left\{\bigsqcup {\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \mid a^{?}\in V\right\}} \\ & = \bigsqcup{\left\{da^{?}\mid a^{?}\in V\right\}} \end{aligned} \]
For uniqueness, suppose that we have two morphisms \(\mathsf{L}{A}\xrightarrow{d,d'}D\) factoring our square in the sense described above and fix \(a^{?}:\mathsf{L}{A}\): \[\begin{aligned} d a^{?} & = d\bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{\bot\right\}}\cup {\left\{\eta_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d\,{\left(\eta_Aa\right)}\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d_\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = \bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{d'\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{d'\,{\left(\eta_Aa\right)}\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = d'\bigsqcup\,{\left({\left\{\bot\right\}} \cup {\left\{\eta_Aa\mid a^{?}=\eta_Aa\right\}}\right)} \\ & = d'a^{?} \end{aligned} \]

mainmatter.p: Let \(A_\bullet\) be a diagram of dcpos indexed in a connected category \(\mathcal{I}\). Then the lifting endofunctor \(\mathsf{L}\) on dcpos preserves the colimit of \(A_\bullet\).

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: Let \(A_\bullet\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{\left\{A_\infty\right\}}\) be a universal cocone; we must show that \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}\) is universal as well, i.e. show any cocone \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{\left\{D\right\}}\) factors uniquely through \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}\) in the following sense:

Using the universal property of \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\) as a co-comma dcpo, we may define \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\xrightarrow{d_\infty}D\) to be the universal map determined by a certain lax square in the following configuration:

First we define \({\mathbf{1}_{}}\xrightarrow{h_\bot}{D}\) to be the element \(d_i\bot\) determined by an arbitrary object \(i\in \mathcal{I}\). To see that this is well-defined, fix \(i,j\in \mathcal{I}\) to check that \(d_i\bot=d_j\bot\). As \(\mathcal{I}\) is connected, we may proceed by induction on a zigzag \(i\leadsto j\in \mathcal{I}\); ultimately, this amounts to checking that for any span \(i\leftarrow k \rightarrow j\) in \(\mathcal{I}\), we have \(d_i\bot=d_k\bot=d_j\bot\). This follows from the strictness of lifted morphisms as well as the naturality of the cocone \(d_\bullet\).
Next we define \({A_\infty}\xrightarrow{h_{A_\infty}}{D}\) using the universal property of the colimiting cocone \({A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{A_\infty}\):

To arrange \(h_{A_\infty}\) and \(h_\bot\) into the desired lax square, we must check that for all \(a_\infty:A_\infty\) we have \(h_\bot \sqsubseteq h_{A_\infty}a_\infty\). Fixing arbitrary \(i\in \mathcal{I}\) and \(a\in A_i\), it suffices to check that \(h_\bot \sqsubseteq h_{A_\infty}\iota_{i}a\). As \(h_\bot=d_i\bot\) and \(h_{A_\infty}\iota_ia = d_i\eta_{A_i}a\), our goal follows directly from the monotonicity of \({\mathsf{L}{A_i}}\xrightarrow{d_i}{D}\).
Thus we have the desired lax square and a unique morphism \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{h}{D}\) factoring it through the co-comma square that defines \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\):

Our goal is to show that we may choose the universal map \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{d_\infty}{D}\) corresponding to the cocone \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{{\left\{D\right\}}}\) to be the morphism \(h\) defined above. First we must prove that the following triangle commutes:
Using the universal property of the co-comma squares for each \(\mathsf{L}{A_i}\), it suffices to check that two diagrams commute:

li.p: We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting using the strictness of lifted morphisms, this amounts to the established fact that \(h\bot = h_\bot\).
We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting with established equations, it suffices to observe that the outer diagram below commutes:

ol.p: Finally, we must check that any two morphisms \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{k,k'}{D}\) factoring \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{{\left\{D\right\}}}\) through \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}}\) are equal. To that end, we will use the universal property of the co-comma square defining \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\) once more to reduce this to checking that \(k,k'\) agree on \(\bot\) and on \(\eta_{A_\bullet}\). The former follows directly from the strictness of \(\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}\), as we have \(k\bot = k{\left(\mathsf{L} \iota_\bullet\bot\right)}=d_\bullet\bot\). For the latter, we must check that \(k\circ \eta_{A_\infty}=k'\circ \eta_{A_\infty}\); by the universal property of the colimiting cocone \({A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{A_\infty}\), it suffices to check that \(k\circ\eta_{A_\infty}\circ \iota_\bullet = k'\circ\eta_{A_\infty}\circ\iota_\bullet\). By naturality of the unit, these are both equal to \(d_\bullet\circ \eta_{A_\bullet}\).

mainmatter.p: Let \(\mathbb{L}={\left(\mathsf{L},\eta,\mu\right)}\) be the lifting monad on dcpos; the Eilenberg-Moore category \(\mathsf{dcpo}^{\mathbb{L}}\) is closed under connected colimits, and these colimits are created by the forgetful functor \({\mathsf{dcpo}^{\mathbb{L}}}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{U}^{\mathbb{L}}}{\mathsf{dcpo}}\).

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: This follows from the following facts:

ol.li: The category of dcpos is (even constructively) known to be cocomplete.
The forgetful functor \({\mathsf{dcpo}^{\mathbb{L}}}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{U}^{\mathbb{L}}}{\mathsf{dcpo}}\) creates whatever colimits \(\mathsf{L}\) preserves.

mainmatter.p: Let \(A_\bullet\) be a diagram of dcpos indexed in a connected category \(\mathcal{I}\). Then the lifting endofunctor \(\mathsf{L}\) on dcpos preserves the colimit of \(A_\bullet\).

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: Let \(A_\bullet\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{\left\{A_\infty\right\}}\) be a universal cocone; we must show that \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}\) is universal as well, i.e. show any cocone \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{\left\{D\right\}}\) factors uniquely through \(\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}\) in the following sense:

Using the universal property of \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\) as a co-comma dcpo, we may define \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\xrightarrow{d_\infty}D\) to be the universal map determined by a certain lax square in the following configuration:

First we define \({\mathbf{1}_{}}\xrightarrow{h_\bot}{D}\) to be the element \(d_i\bot\) determined by an arbitrary object \(i\in \mathcal{I}\). To see that this is well-defined, fix \(i,j\in \mathcal{I}\) to check that \(d_i\bot=d_j\bot\). As \(\mathcal{I}\) is connected, we may proceed by induction on a zigzag \(i\leadsto j\in \mathcal{I}\); ultimately, this amounts to checking that for any span \(i\leftarrow k \rightarrow j\) in \(\mathcal{I}\), we have \(d_i\bot=d_k\bot=d_j\bot\). This follows from the strictness of lifted morphisms as well as the naturality of the cocone \(d_\bullet\).
Next we define \({A_\infty}\xrightarrow{h_{A_\infty}}{D}\) using the universal property of the colimiting cocone \({A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{A_\infty}\):

To arrange \(h_{A_\infty}\) and \(h_\bot\) into the desired lax square, we must check that for all \(a_\infty:A_\infty\) we have \(h_\bot \sqsubseteq h_{A_\infty}a_\infty\). Fixing arbitrary \(i\in \mathcal{I}\) and \(a\in A_i\), it suffices to check that \(h_\bot \sqsubseteq h_{A_\infty}\iota_{i}a\). As \(h_\bot=d_i\bot\) and \(h_{A_\infty}\iota_ia = d_i\eta_{A_i}a\), our goal follows directly from the monotonicity of \({\mathsf{L}{A_i}}\xrightarrow{d_i}{D}\).
Thus we have the desired lax square and a unique morphism \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{h}{D}\) factoring it through the co-comma square that defines \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\):

Our goal is to show that we may choose the universal map \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{d_\infty}{D}\) corresponding to the cocone \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{{\left\{D\right\}}}\) to be the morphism \(h\) defined above. First we must prove that the following triangle commutes:
Using the universal property of the co-comma squares for each \(\mathsf{L}{A_i}\), it suffices to check that two diagrams commute:

li.p: We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting using the strictness of lifted morphisms, this amounts to the established fact that \(h\bot = h_\bot\).
We must check that the outer triangle below commutes:
Rewriting with established equations, it suffices to observe that the outer diagram below commutes:

ol.p: Finally, we must check that any two morphisms \({\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}}\xrightarrow{k,k'}{D}\) factoring \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{d_\bullet}{{\left\{D\right\}}}\) through \({\mathsf{L}{A_\bullet}}\xrightarrow{\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}}{{\left\{\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\right\}}}\) are equal. To that end, we will use the universal property of the co-comma square defining \(\mathsf{L}{A_\infty}\) once more to reduce this to checking that \(k,k'\) agree on \(\bot\) and on \(\eta_{A_\bullet}\). The former follows directly from the strictness of \(\mathsf{L}{\iota_\bullet}\), as we have \(k\bot = k{\left(\mathsf{L} \iota_\bullet\bot\right)}=d_\bullet\bot\). For the latter, we must check that \(k\circ \eta_{A_\infty}=k'\circ \eta_{A_\infty}\); by the universal property of the colimiting cocone \({A_\bullet}\xrightarrow{\iota_\bullet}{A_\infty}\), it suffices to check that \(k\circ\eta_{A_\infty}\circ \iota_\bullet = k'\circ\eta_{A_\infty}\circ\iota_\bullet\). By naturality of the unit, these are both equal to \(d_\bullet\circ \eta_{A_\bullet}\).
